title: malus zonalis
intro: Malus zonalis, also known as the Chinese crabapple, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to China and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Malus zonalis can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
